access_status.notes: Rituxan is non-preferred; Truxima, Riabni, and Ruxience are preferred formulary alternatives.

covered_indications.criteria: Includes Granulomatosis with polyangiitis (Wegener’s) or microscopic polyangiitis. Must be used in combination with glucocorticoids. Induction requires disease control or 16 weeks between courses for follow-up.
Initiated in combination with a corticosteroid unless contraindicated. Subsequent infusions administered no sooner than 16 weeks following the previous course.

Adults with moderately to severely active disease. Must have tried one conventional synthetic DMARD (e.g., methotrexate) for 3 months or a biologic. Not used concurrently with another biologic or targeted synthetic DMARD.
Prescribed by or in consultation with a hematologist.
Patient has chronic graft-versus-host disease and has tried at least one systemic medication (e.g., corticosteroids, ruxolitinib, belumosudil).
Used as part of a conditioning regimen for allogeneic transplant.
Patient has tried one other therapy (e.g., IVIG, anti-D, corticosteroids, eltrombopag). At least 6 months between treatment courses for repeat use.

covered_indications.condition: Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors
covered_indications.criteria: Toxicity developed while receiving a checkpoint inhibitor (e.g., pembrolizumab, nivolumab) and patient is symptomatic despite a trial of at least one systemic corticosteroid.

covered_indications.condition: Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease (SARD-ILD)
covered_indications.criteria: Patient is ≥ 18 years of age; diagnosis confirmed by high-resolution computed tomography. 24 weeks or greater between treatment courses.
Patient is at moderate or high risk for progressive loss of kidney function.
Used for frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent disease.
Inadequate efficacy or significant intolerance to at least TWO other disease-modifying agents. Not used concurrently with another MS disease-modifying agent. At least 6 months between courses.
Confirmed anti-muscle-specific tyrosine kinase (MuSK) antibody-positive. Tried pyridostigmine and at least one immunosuppressant therapy (e.g., azathioprine, cyclosporine).
Prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist.
Patient is ≤ 18 years of age. Tried at least one systemic corticosteroid or one glucocorticoid-sparing agent (e.g., tacrolimus, cyclophosphamide).
Used for desensitization therapy prior to/immediately after transplant or for antibody-mediated rejection.
Tried at least one standard immunomodulating or immunosuppressant agent (e.g., hydroxychloroquine, methotrexate). 6 months or greater between treatment courses.
Used in combination with systemic corticosteroids and therapeutic plasma exchange.

criteria: Must be prescribed by or in consultation with a physician specializing in the condition being treated.

For Rituxan requests: Documentation required that patient has tried Truxima, Riabni, AND Ruxience and cannot continue due to formulation differences in inactive ingredients resulting in allergy or serious adverse reaction.
Approval for Rheumatoid Arthritis requires a 3-month trial of one conventional synthetic DMARD unless a biologic has already been tried.
Approvals for MS require trial of two other disease-modifying agents.
Subsequent course approvals require documented beneficial clinical response or improvement in symptoms.

steps.notes: Required before Rituxan (brand) will be covered.

steps.required_drug_or_class: One conventional synthetic DMARD (e.g., methotrexate, leflunomide, hydroxychloroquine, or sulfasalazine)
steps.notes: Required for Rheumatoid Arthritis initial therapy unless a biologic has already been tried.

steps.required_drug_or_class: Two other disease-modifying agents for multiple sclerosis
steps.notes: Required for Multiple Sclerosis initial therapy.

limits.value: 375 mg/m2 per dose for 4 doses separated by at least 7 days
limits.notes: ANCA induction, AIHA, GVHD, ITP, Immunotherapy toxicities, Membranous Nephropathy, Minimal Change Disease, NMOSD, Pediatric Nephrotic Syndrome, Myasthenia Gravis, TTP.

limits.value: 1,000 mg intravenous doses separated by 2 weeks (2 doses per course)
limits.notes: ANCA induction, Pemphigus Vulgaris, RA, ILD, Membranous Nephropathy, Minimal Change Disease, NMOSD, Myasthenia Gravis.

limits.value: 100 mg administered intravenously with doses separated by at least 7 days for 4 doses
limits.notes: Alternative dosing for Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia.
ANCA Follow-up for patients < 18 years of age.

limits.value: 2,000 mg total administered as one or two infusions over 1 month

limits.notes: ANCA follow-up, Pemphigus Vulgaris, Rheumatoid Arthritis.

policy_metadata.policy_name: Rituximab Intravenous Products for Non-Oncology Indications

access_status.notes: Truxima, Riabni, and Ruxience are preferred over Rituxan for both Employer and Individual and Family Plans.

covered_indications.criteria: Includes granulomatosis with polyangiitis or microscopic polyangiitis. Must be used in combination with glucocorticoids. Requires prescription by or in consultation with a rheumatologist, nephrologist, pulmonologist, or immunologist.
Adults with moderate to severe disease. Initial therapy must be in combination with a corticosteroid unless contraindicated. Prescribed by or in consultation with a dermatologist.

Moderately to severely active disease in adults. Must be used in combination with methotrexate. Patient must have had an inadequate response to one or more tumor necrosis factor inhibitors or a trial of one conventional synthetic DMARD.
Prescribed by or in consultation with a hematologist.

Chronic GVHD. Patient must have tried at least one systemic medication (e.g., corticosteroids, ruxolitinib, etc.). Prescribed by or in consultation with an oncologist, hematologist, or transplant physician.
Used as part of a conditioning regimen for allogeneic transplant. Prescribed by or in consultation with an oncologist, hematologist, or transplant physician.
Patient has tried one other therapy (e.g., IVIG, anti-D, corticosteroids, etc.). Prescribed by or in consultation with a hematologist.

covered_indications.condition: Immunotherapy-Related Toxicities Associated with Checkpoint Inhibitors
covered_indications.criteria: Toxicity developed while receiving a checkpoint inhibitor. Symptomatic despite a trial of at least one systemic corticosteroid. Prescribed by or in consultation with an appropriate specialist.

covered_indications.condition: Interstitial Lung Disease Associated with Systemic Autoimmune Rheumatic Disease (SARD-ILD)
covered_indications.criteria: Patient ≥ 18 years of age. Diagnosis confirmed by high-resolution computed tomography. Prescribed by or in consultation with a pulmonologist or rheumatologist.
Patient at moderate or high risk for progressive loss of kidney function. Prescribed by or in consultation with a nephrologist.
Frequently relapsing or steroid-dependent disease. Prescribed by or in consultation with a nephrologist.
Inadequate efficacy or significant intolerance to at least TWO other disease-modifying agents. Prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist.

Confirmed anti-muscle-specific tyrosine kinase antibody-positive disease. Trial of pyridostigmine and at least one immunosuppressant therapy required. Prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist.
Prescribed by or in consultation with a neurologist.
Patient ≤ 18 years of age. Trial of at least one systemic corticosteroid or glucocorticoid-sparing agent. Prescribed by or in consultation with a nephrologist.
Used for desensitization therapy or antibody-mediated rejection. Prescribed by or in consultation with a transplant physician.
Trial of at least one standard immunomodulating or immunosuppressant agent. Prescribed by or in consultation with a rheumatologist, nephrologist, or neurologist.
Used in combination with systemic corticosteroids and therapeutic plasma exchange. Prescribed by or in consultation with a hematologist.

criteria: Medication must be prescribed by or in consultation with a physician who specializes in the condition being treated.

For Rituxan: Patient must have tried and failed all biosimilar alternatives (Truxima, Riabni, and Ruxience) due to formulation differences in inactive ingredients that would result in a significant allergy or serious adverse reaction.
Approval requires meeting specific clinical criteria for the requested indication (e.g., trial of prior therapies, specialist consultation, specific diagnosis confirmation).
Documentation of trial of prior therapies and clinical response is required for many indications.
Minimum time intervals between treatment courses must be observed (e.g., 16 weeks for RA/Pemphigus, 6 months for MS/SLE/ITP).

steps.notes: Required step for brand Rituxan coverage; patient must try all three biosimilars first.

steps.required_drug_or_class: Condition-specific standard therapies (e.g., DMARDs for RA, corticosteroids for ITP, disease-modifying agents for MS)
steps.notes: Specific step requirements vary by indication (e.g., trial of at least TWO agents for Multiple Sclerosis).

limits.notes: Commonly administered weekly for 4 doses across multiple indications (ANCA, AIHA, ITP, etc.).
Commonly administered as two doses separated by 2 weeks (RA, ANCA, Pemphigus, ILD, etc.).
Maintenance dose for Pemphigus Vulgaris every 6 months.
Low-dose option for Autoimmune Hemolytic Anemia.
Total dose administered over 1 month for Multiple Sclerosis.
Follow-up dose for pediatric ANCA-associated vasculitis.

policy_metadata.policy_name: Rituximab Intravenous Products for Non-Oncology Indications

access_status.notes: Riabni, Ruxience, and Truxima are the preferred rituximab products. Rituxan and other rituximab products are non-preferred.

covered_indications.criteria: Documented platelet count < 30 x 10^9/L; and history of failure, contraindication, or intolerance to one of the following: Anti-D immunoglobulin, Corticosteroids, Immune globulin, Thrombopoietin receptor agonist (TPO-RA), or Splenectomy.
Diagnosis of moderate to severe pemphigus vulgaris.

Diagnosis of Wegener’s granulomatosis or microscopic polyangiitis; and patient is receiving concurrent therapy with glucocorticoids or has a history of contraindication/intolerance to glucocorticoids.
Diagnosis of autoimmune hemolytic anemia, including chronic cold agglutinin disease.

Moderate to severe disease activity; and history of failure, contraindication, or intolerance to at least one TNF inhibitor; and patient is receiving concurrent therapy with methotrexate or has contraindication/intolerance; and patient is not receiving rituximab in combination with a targeted immunomodulator.
Diagnosis of PTLD.

Diagnosis confirmed by a neurologist; serologic testing for anti-AQP4-IgG antibodies performed; specific clinical history (optic neuritis, acute myelitis, etc.); multiple sclerosis ruled out; and not receiving in combination with MS disease-modifying therapies, Anti-IL6 therapy, or complement inhibitors.

Recent immunotherapy treatment with a checkpoint inhibitor; and limited/no improvement after glucocorticoids (min 7 days) or contraindication; or positive for autoimmune encephalopathy antibody with infectious causes ruled out.
Used in combination with plasma exchange therapy; and receiving concurrent therapy with glucocorticoids or history of contraindication/intolerance.

Diagnosis of primary progressive multiple sclerosis (PPMS) or relapsing forms of MS; and patient is not receiving rituximab in combination with other MS disease modifying therapies, B cell targeted therapy, or lymphocyte trafficking blockers.

Diagnosis confirmed by ACR/EULAR classification (involvement of 1+ organ, exclusion criteria negative, inclusion criteria positive); prescribed by/in consultation with a specialist; and not in combination with Uplizna.

criteria: For initial therapy, prescriber attests dosing is in accordance with FDA approved labeling or published clinical evidence.
For continuation of therapy, documentation of a positive clinical response is required.
For non-preferred products (Rituxan or other biosimilars), history of intolerance, contraindication, or serious adverse event to Riabni, Ruxience, OR Truxima is required.
Physician attests that the same intolerance, contraindication, or serious adverse event would not be expected to occur with the respective non-preferred product.

steps.notes: Preferred products must be tried before Rituxan or other non-listed rituximab products.

steps.required_drug_or_class: Anti-D immunoglobulin, Corticosteroids, Immune globulin, TPO-RA, or Splenectomy
steps.notes: Required for Immune thrombocytopenic purpura (ITP) indication.

payer_name: Priority Health Commercial and MyPriority Plans

covered_indications.criteria: No prior authorization required

criteria: No PA required for ICD-10 code C50

payer_name: Priority Health Commercial and MyPriority Plans

access_status.notes: Rituxan and Rituxan Hycela are Not Covered. Preferred alternatives include Riabni (rituximab-arrx), Ruxience (rituximab-pvvr), and Truxima (rituximab-abbs).

steps.required_drug_or_class: Q5123 - Riabni (rituximab-arrx), Q5119 - Ruxience (rituximab-pvvr), or Q5115 - Truxima (rituximab-abbs)
steps.notes: Covered alternatives required before use of Rituxan or Rituxan Hycela.

site_of_care.notes: Site of Service applies to Truxima, Ruxience, and Riabni.
